title: Jamaican Grilled Duck
description: A sweet and spicy Jamaican-inspired dish featuring grilled duck breast. This recipe is perfect for a special occasion or a summer barbecue.
instructions: Season the duck breast with Jamaican jerk seasoning, making sure to coat all surfaces evenly.
Grill the duck breast for 5-7 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F.
Transfer the duck breast to a plate and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, grill the pineapple slices for 2-3 minutes per side, or until they have nice char marks.
Thinly slice the grilled pineapple and red onion.
Slice the rested duck breast into thin slices.
In a small bowl, whisk together lime juice and brown sugar (if using).
To assemble the dish, place a few slices of duck breast on a plate, then top with grilled pineapple, red onion, and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.
Drizzle the lime juice mixture over the top of the dish and serve immediately.
